name.tr: İş Arkadaşlarına Yönelik Yüzeysel Davranma Ölçeği

construct.en: Degree of suppressing real emotions in coworker interactions
construct.tr: İş arkadaşlarıyla etkileşimlerde gerçek duyguları bastırma düzeyi

original_citation.en: Ozcelik, H. (2013). An empirical analysis of surface acting in intra-organizational relationships. Journal of Organizational Behavior, 34(3), 291–309. https://doi.org/10.1002/job.1798
original_citation.tr: Ozcelik, H. (2013). An empirical analysis of surface acting in intra-organizational relationships. Journal of Organizational Behavior, 34(3), 291–309. https://doi.org/10.1002/job.1798

description.en: Regulating emotional displays toward coworkers by suppressing true feelings and showing emotions that are not genuinely felt.
description.tr: İş arkadaşlarına yönelik duygusal ifadeleri, gerçek duyguları bastırarak ve gerçekte hissedilmeyen duyguları göstererek düzenleme.

response_format_tr: 5’li Likert tipi ölçek, 1 = Hiç katılmıyorum ile 5 = Kesinlikle katılıyorum
response_format_en: 5-point Likert scale, from 1 = strongly disagree to 5 = strongly agree.
target_population_tr: Working adults in coworker or work-unit interaction contexts.
target_population_en: İş arkadaşlarıyla ya da iş birimi içinde etkileşim yaşayan çalışan yetişkinler.

reliability.en: Reliability was acceptable; the single-level alpha was 0.84, and the work-unit-level alpha was .89.
reliability.tr: Tek düzeyli alfa 0.84, iş birimi düzeyindeki alfa ise .89 olarak raporlanmıştır.

validity_notes.en: CFA results supported a one-factor structure. The single-level CFA showed acceptable fit, and the multilevel CFA also showed good fit after allowing a residual covariance between Items 4 and 5.
validity_notes.tr: Tek düzeyli CFA kabul edilebilir uyum göstermiş, 4. ve 5. maddeler arasında artık kovaryansı tanımlandıktan sonra çok düzeyli CFA da iyi uyum vermiştir.

citation.en: Peker, M., & Ersoy, N. C. (2023). Disentangling person and work unit level relationships between a climate of authenticity and work engagement: The mediating role of surface acting in interactions with work unit members. Current Psychology, 42, 26317–26330. https://doi.org/10.1007/s12144-022-03683-x
citation.tr: Peker, M., & Ersoy, N. C. (2023). Disentangling person and work unit level relationships between a climate of authenticity and work engagement: The mediating role of surface acting in interactions with work unit members. Current Psychology, 42, 26317–26330. https://doi.org/10.1007/s12144-022-03683-x
